title: SpaceX Seeks $75 Billion in Record IPO to Fund AI, Launch
description: SpaceX is seeking to raise $75 billion in an initial public offering that would be the biggest of all time, as Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence company targets a historic debut that could clear a path for more mega-listings.

title: DoubleLine’s Robert Cohen on AI Debt Loads, Private Credit
description: Robert Cohen, Director of Global Developed Credit at DoubleLine, discusses the current corporate fundraising environment. He notes that despite ongoing capital raising activities in both debt and equity markets, companies—particularly high-quality ones—continue to maintain strong balance sheets and generate healthy cash flow. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fmr. SEC Chair: Giants Like SpaceX Need To Find Revenue Model
description: Gary Gensler, former chair of both the CFTC and SEC, discusses the capital markets and the regulatory challenges they face. The discussion highlights the anticipated surge in IPO activity in 2026, with reported estimates of capital raised reaching up to $75 billion. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
